verses.text: These are the journeys of Israel which went forth out of the land of Egypt by their armies under Moses and Aaron. Moses wrote their goings out by the commandment of the LORD; these are their journeys. Guidance: Moses keeps a travel log. Every campsite — even the bitter ones — gets a line. God wanted the wanderings remembered, not erased. The maps of our wilderness years are not failures; they are the route by which we arrived at the river.

These are the journeys of Israel which went forth out of the land of Egypt by their armies under Moses and Aaron. Moses wrote their goings out by the commandment of the LORD; these are their journeys. Guidance: Moses keeps a travel log. Every campsite — even the bitter ones — gets a line. God wanted the wanderings remembered, not erased. The maps of our wilderness years are not failures; they are the route by which we arrived at the river.

From Rameses the day after Passover, the children of Israel went out with a high hand. Succoth, Etham, Pi-hahiroth — through the sea — Marah, Elim with its twelve springs and seventy palms, by the Red Sea, the wilderness of Sin, Dophkah, Alush, Rephidim (no water), the wilderness of Sinai. Guidance: From the night of slain firstborns to the mount of the law — fourteen stations. Egypt to Sinai in one read-through. Israel's God can move a nation from bricks to thunder in a couple of months when He wills. The wilderness was not always slow.

Israel's God can move a nation from bricks to thunder in a couple of months when He wills. The wilderness was not always slow.

Sinai to Kibroth-hattaavah (where the greedy were buried), Hazeroth, Rithmah, Rimmon-parez, Libnah, Rissah, Kehelathah, Shapher, Haradah, Makheloth, Tahath, Tarah, Mithcah, Hashmonah, Moseroth, Bene-jaakan, Hor-hagidgad, Jotbathah, Ebronah, Ezion-gaber, and Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin. Guidance: A litany of unfamiliar names — the long middle of a story we tend to skip. But God writes them all down. The forgotten stops matter as much as the famous ones. Where He brought you in year fourteen still counts in year forty.

From Kadesh to mount Hor — there Aaron died, the first day of the fifth month of the fortieth year, age 123. Then Zalmonah, Punon, Oboth, Ije-abarim, Dibon-gad, Almon-diblathaim, the mountains of Abarim before Nebo, and the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho. Guidance: One sentence and Aaron is gone — exact date, exact age. The text marks the priest's death like a stone in the road. After Hor, the names become familiar from chapters 21 onward. The end of the journey is in sight; only one river left.

The LORD in the plains of Moab: when ye pass over Jordan, drive out all the inhabitants, destroy their pictures, molten images, high places. Dispossess them — I have given you the land. Divide it by lot. If ye will not drive them out, those that remain shall be pricks in your eyes, thorns in your sides; I shall do unto you, as I thought to do unto them. Guidance: A solemn warning before the conquest. Half-obedience would not work. Leave the idols and they will recruit your children. Pricks and thorns — what you tolerate today will be the splinter under tomorrow's skin.

The LORD in the plains of Moab: when ye pass over Jordan, drive out all the inhabitants, destroy their pictures, molten images, high places. Dispossess them — I have given you the land. Divide it by lot. If ye will not drive them out, those that remain shall be pricks in your eyes, thorns in your sides; I shall do unto you, as I thought to do unto them. Guidance: A solemn warning before the conquest. Half-obedience would not work. Leave the idols and they will recruit your children. Pricks and thorns — what you tolerate today will be the splinter under tomorrow's skin.
